title: psoralea rigidula
intro: Psoralea rigidula, also known as Wolf's Claws or False Indigo, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate the growth of new hair and skin, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Psoralea rigidula include improved hair growth, skin rejuvenation, and enhanced immune function, as well as potential benefits for cardiovascular health and cancer prevention. Psoralea rigidula is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and can also be applied topically as an oil or cream for skin and hair care.
